description_long: This is a large, leafless deciduous tree with a thick, gnarled trunk and an extensive network of bare branches radiating outward in irregular directions. The bark appears rough and textured, with a dark brown to grayish coloration, giving the tree an aged and weathered look. The base of the trunk is wide and flares out into visible root structures that anchor the tree to the ground. The branches are twisted and uneven, some extending horizontally while others reach upward or downward, creating a dramatic silhouette. There are no visible leaves or foliage, emphasizing the stark, skeletal form of the tree. This type of tree is commonly found in urban parks, abandoned lots, or as a striking feature in landscaped environments.
description: A large, leafless, gnarled tree with a thick trunk, rough bark, and sprawling, twisted branches. The base flares out with visible roots, giving it a dramatic, aged appearance.
description_view_0: The tree is seen from the front, highlighting the thick trunk, flared base, and several prominent branches extending outward and upward.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the asymmetry of the branches, with some extending far to the left and right, and the trunk's rough texture is clearly visible.
description_view_2: From this angle, the tree's wide horizontal spread is most apparent, with branches reaching far out to the sides and the trunk standing tall in the center.
description_view_3: A rear-angled view shows the twisting nature of the branches and the flared base, with the trunk's curvature and root structure visible.
